questions.question: Which force arises when a body is immersed in a fluid and displaces the fluid?

questions.option_1: To measure the difference in potential between two points
questions.option_2: To allow maximum current through it

questions.question: Which component is commonly used in a circuit to vary the resistance?
A body of mass 10 kg is moving with a uniform velocity of 5 m/s. What is the net force acting on it?
A gas stove flame burns blue because of:
questions.option_2: Presence of hydrogen in the air

questions.question: Which force acts without physical contact and governs celestial body motion in mechanics?
Which of the following is NOT a thermometric property?
Which of the following modern physicist closely examined Newtonian mechanics’ foundations?

What is the dimensional formula of pressure?
According to the second law of reflection, all the incident ray, the normal to the mirror at the point of incidence and the reflected ray:
questions.option_2: are mutually perpendicular to each other

questions.question: Commemorated on National Science Day, CV Raman established key scientific platforms in India. The scientific journal founded by him was _______.
The coordination number of atoms in a body-centered cubic (BCC) lattice is:
Which of the following is the SI unit for sound intensity?
A sound wave with a period of 0.01 seconds has a frequency of:
What is required for the generation of a sound wave?
questions.option_2: Vibration and an elastic medium only

questions.question: The Meissner effect is a defining characteristic of which state of matter?
What happens to magnetic fields when a material becomes superconducting and experiences the Meissner effect?
questions.option_1: Magnetic fields are increased in strength within the material.
questions.option_2: Magnetic fields are completely expelled from the interior of the material.
questions.option_3: Magnetic fields are absorbed and stored inside the material.
questions.option_4: Magnetic fields penetrate the material freely.
